title: Garlicky Peruvian Beef Anticuchos
description: A simple, weeknight-friendly Peruvian dish featuring beef anticuchos with a garlicky yuzu-cure. Perfect for a dinner or lunch option.

ingredients.name: yuzu juice
ingredients.notes: use a yuzu or a combination of equal parts lemon and grapefruit juice if needed

instructions: Preheat a grill or grill pan to medium-high heat.
Cut the beef heart into 1-inch chunks and set aside.
In a blender or food processor, blend together yuzu juice, soy sauce, garlic, black pepper, and a pinch of salt until smooth.
Thread the beef chunks onto skewers, leaving a little space between each piece.
Brush the yuzu-cure onto the meat, making sure each piece is coated evenly.
Grill the anticuchos for 4-5 minutes per side, or until they reach an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C).
Meanwhile, heat the lard or oil in a large skillet over medium heat.
Add the sliced onion and cook for 4-5 minutes, or until softened and lightly caramelized.
Add the diced chili peppers and cook for an additional minute.
Serve the grilled anticuchos hot with the caramelized onion mixture, garnished with chopped cilantro.
Adjust seasoning as needed, to taste.
